opp.name: Khansaheb
opp.next_step: Confidence 50% 18th May - Met the HOP/Champion (Abraham, - Bilal the EB was unavailable) - he confirmed we are shortlisted against one other provider, their current thinking is that all non-essential spend is paused until later in the year whilst the conflict/blockade continues (new construction projects are paused, though they have several years of projects underway) - their current concern is the materials prices have sky-rocketed due to the blockade & fuel prices - I have planted some seeds around my blog post on investing in procuretech to protect their margins & unlock better materials pricing), offered to present to upper mgt and/or bring Carl in in helpful to show how we can help strengthen their economics, he will talk to Bilal. 16th April - Project continues to be on pause due to the war, but expecting to resume, confirmed they had shortlisted two vendors and Zycus is one, (wouldn't say who else) - current expectation is that they will resume in Q2 - moving back to end Q2. 25th Mar - Response that the project is paused due to the war priorities: " Given the present conditions, we have a delay in the closure of this project. We will provide further updates should there be any changes." appears we are still in the mix as one of their options. 13th Mar - Reached out again for an update (several out of office responses) and requested a meeting after the Eid break. 17th Feb - Same response, not interested in meeting up... "Thank you for your patience, the project is still on and under review with our consultant . The team will reach out to you as & when there are new developments". 16th Feb - Gone quiet since they refocussed on a reduced scope, escalated to senior EB requesting a f2f this week, reducing confidence to 5%. 16th Jan - Pushing to get a meeting while Tanya is in town end Jan/early Feb, Abraham will get back to us. 20th Dec - Pushed a couple of times for updates - response 'management are evaluating, will update in the new year' 19th Nov - As expected, when they provided the usage for the pilot it's too low, rather than walk away I have added some modules they were interested in and pitched 2 scenarios as our 'min prepared to support', still a small deal but could be acceptable, let's see their response, if negative its dead. Dropped confidence to 5%. 30th Oct - Commercial discussion held, changes in approach, now looking to run a pilot approach to a business unit before deploying to wider business , reduces the scope and also changed sourcing to a later phase - now on the cusp of being too small, will get clarity once there are eInvoicing estimates of usage - moved close date back to Q4 and confidence down to 10% 28th Oct - Call scheduled Thurs 30th to discuss commercial estimation. 22nd Oct - Nudged to see if they need help with completing the commercial estimation together. 14th Oct - POC went well, they are asking for pricing, have shared the estimation template and offer to run through it together. 13th Oct - POC with Bilal (Exec Sponsor) confirmed onsite scheduled for 14th Oct (with Tanya & Dan onsite) 7th Oct - POC held onsite with Khansaheb senior manager (Abraham) and Crowe consultants, IF successful they will escalate to another session with Bilal (Exec sponsor) 23rd Sept - Pre-POC meeting set for 29th to review POC prior to live demo session (later that week TBC). 11th Sept - POC details shared to run a live demo .... pilots team tied up for next few weeks Tanya will have to build delaying the process. 25th Aug - No response from Ravi, called and postponed (also not clear Abraham is back yet) - some option offered, no response yet. 14th Aug - Ravi scheduled 26th but we have a clash, requested to reschedule. 7th Aug - Demo'ed to Bilal (GM) and exec sponsor, went well, very engaged - next step to build a POC demo based on their processes when Abrahim returns on 26th (maybe run it on 10th when Tanya/Carl are in Dubai? TBC). 31st July - Ravi called to say that the GM (Bilal) doesn't want to wait for Abraham's response - demo being arranged for next Weds-Fri. 29th July - Presented to Abraham (HOP), went well, feedback was positive, next steps are: follow up demo on invoicing & Sourcing, to sign an NDA, to get a proposal and present to management prior to a decision, shared the pricing estimate template and NDA underway. - moved to Formal Evaluation. 22nd July - Following demo request had a disco call with their consultant (Ravi - Crowe UAE) managing the project, interested in S2C but priority focus is supplier management- next step is to present to head of procurement, will be presenting iSupplier/ZSN, iRisk and iSource- scheduled for 29th Jul20
opp.ais_why:
• Per the 16 Apr 2026 rep note from Dan Quinn: 'Project continues to be on pause due to the war, but expecting to resume, confirmed they had shortlisted two vendors and Zycus is one... current expectation is that they will resume in Q2.' Per 17 Feb 2026: 'Not interested in meeting up... "Thank you for your patience, the project is still on and under review with our consultant. The team will reach out to you as & when there are new developments".' Abraham Mathew is shortlisting Zycus but is not engaged on any AI conversation.
• Per the 19 Nov 2025 rep note: 'As expected, when they provided the usage for the pilot it's too low, rather than walk away I have added some modules they were interested in and pitched 2 scenarios as our "min prepared to support", still a small deal but could be acceptable.' Product scope is iSource;iSupplier;eInvoicing;iRisk - foundational only, no Merlin/Agentic/ANA. Avoma meetings (9 total, khansaheb.com domain) focus on Crowe-led RFP mechanics, not AI.
• High confidence: 9 valid Avoma meetings on Khansaheb domain, products in scope have no AI component, BD = Unengaged, project on pause due to war. Multiple rep notes spanning Nov 2025-Apr 2026 contain zero references to Merlin/Agentic/AI from any named Khansaheb stakeholder. Clear AI Resistant.

items.quote: no cost-per-invoice, cycle-time or savings figure has been agreed

items.area: Identity of the second shortlisted vendor
items.quote: confirmed they had shortlisted two vendors and Zycus is one (wouldn't say who else)
all non-essential spend is paused until later in the year

metrics.narrative: Value drivers are well articulated qualitatively but never quantified. The Oct 2025 POC and commercial call confirmed scope across supplier onboarding, eInvoicing (AI OCR capture and 3-way matching) and Merlin intake, targeting automation of manual vendor onboarding and invoice processing. Since the spend freeze the live metric is margin protection: per Next Step 18 May 2026 the buyer's stated concern is that 'materials prices have sky-rocketed due to the blockade and fuel prices', and Dan planted the procuretech-to-protect-margins angle. No cost-per-invoice, cycle-time or savings figure has been agreed; building a quantified ROI case the champion can carry internally is the open move that unfreezes budget.

champion.narrative: Abraham Mathew (Head of Procurement) is a developing-to-solid champion: he led the project from the first demo (29 Jul 2025), drove detailed POC configuration feedback (30 Sep 2025), agreed to share sample vendor documents, and met Dan face-to-face on 18 May 2026 when no other contact would. His advocacy is currently constrained: his 19 May 2026 email was honest but passive ('wait and watch, I will get back to you'), he lacks authority to unfreeze budget, and the deal is single-threaded to him. What has developed is consistent access and hosting; what is still missing is a secured internal Bilal/management meeting for Zycus and a quantified case he can sell upward.

competition.narrative: Zycus is one of two shortlisted vendors (Next Step 16 April 2026, 'wouldn't say who else'), but the rival's identity has never been disclosed, so it cannot be directly counter-positioned. The real live competitor is do-nothing / deferral: the buyer has paused all non-essential spend and frames procurement automation as discretionary while the conflict continues. No active bake-off is running while spend is frozen, so the unnamed peer is a dormant secondary threat. The wedge is to reframe procuretech as a margin-protection lever during the downturn (Dan's blog and Agentic AI summit content shared May 2026) backed by the validated POC; surfacing the rival's identity via the Crowe consultants is an opportunistic, lower-priority move.

sources: Avoma commercial call 30 Oct 2025 (Abraham Mathew, Ravi Shankar)
identify_pain.narrative: Confirmed operational pain: manual vendor onboarding with compliance-document validation (trade-license, VAT and Emirates ID expiry) across multiple entities on different ERPs, manual invoice processing, and no real-time supplier visibility. On 30 Oct 2025 Abraham and Ravi detailed the multi-entity, multi-ERP complexity ('the divisions run on different ERPs, the books are different') and government-portal integration failures they want minimized. The overlay pain since the blockade is financial: spiking materials and fuel prices squeezing margins (Next Step 18 May 2026). Pain owner is Abraham for operations; the margin and economics concern is owned by Bilal and management, which is exactly where the value case must land.

paper_process.narrative: Contracting mechanics are partly surfaced. On 29 Jul 2025 next steps included signing an NDA, receiving a proposal and presenting to management, with the NDA 'underway'. Commercial structure is clear from 30 Oct 2025: minimum three-year term (typical five) because SaaS cost is front-loaded, bundle discounts only if iSourcing is taken as an immediate Phase 2, and pricing dependent on confirmed user counts (two admin, five power, one business user) and invoice volumes. A regulatory dependency gates eInvoicing paper: it must integrate with the UAE FTA portal via a pre-approved intermediary. No redlining, MSA or signature stage has been reached because budget is frozen; visibility returns only when management approves the unfreeze.

economic_buyer.narrative: The economic buyer is Bilal Hamadeh (GM and Exec Sponsor per the Next Step log), with management approval required for the pilot spend. Bilal engaged early (demo 7 Aug 2025 'very engaged'; onsite POC 13-14 Oct 2025) but has not joined a working session since Oct 2025 and was explicitly unavailable for the 14 and 18 May 2026 meetings. On the 30 Oct 2025 call Ravi confirmed the authority chain: 'Ultimately it goes to Bilal, and Bilal will approach management for this pilot project.' Budget sits with Bilal and management and is frozen; the deal is single-threaded to Abraham who cannot release it, so re-engaging Bilal on restart timing is the priority.

decision_process.narrative: Partner-led, phased evaluation read from the call sequence: discovery with Crowe consultant Ravi Shankar (22 Jul 2025), HOP demo to Abraham (29 Jul), GM/Exec-Sponsor demo to Bilal (7 Aug), configuration review (30 Sep), onsite POC with Bilal and Crowe (7-14 Oct), then commercials (30 Oct). On 30 Oct 2025 the buyer reshaped into a phased delivery, Phase 1 being an iSupplier and eInvoicing pilot on a limited set of entities with sourcing deferred, explicitly pending management review through Bilal. Ravi stated 'every pilot project goes to management for review and approval'. The process is now stalled at management review pending an external, date-uncertain spend-freeze decision.

decision_criteria.narrative: Criteria are well documented from the demo and POC cycle: a vendor portal with self-registration plus KYC/compliance document validation (trade-license, VAT and ICV certificate checks with expiry alerts), configurable role-based access, 3-way invoice matching, AI OCR invoice capture, and phased ERP integration. On the 14 Oct 2025 in-person demo Tanya walked the confirmed Khansaheb onboarding flow (VRF, duplicate check, conditional approvals, risk assessment) as 'absolutely in scope'. eInvoicing adds a hard regulatory criterion: compliance with the UAE FTA e-invoicing 5-corner model via an approved intermediary, raised by Ravi and Abraham on 30 Oct 2025.

cro_panel.intro: One read per score, in language a CRO/CO can act on. It's genuinely competitive and could go either way; the reads below say where it stands.

blocks.how: A deal at 'Shortlisted' anchors near 55. Thin signals pull the read to 52.
blocks.title: Zycus win position

bullets.full: Champion is driving it — Abraham Mathew is a consistent, engaged champion who hosts Dan and drives feedback, but is currently passive ('wait and watch'), lacks budget authority, and has not yet secured a management meeting for Zycus.
bullets.text: Champion is driving it — Abraham Mathew is a consistent, engaged champion who hosts Dan and drives feedback, but is currently passive ('wait and watch'), lacks budget…

blocks.read: The threat is the deal stalling into 'do nothing', not a competitor beating us.
blocks.title: What could lose it
blocks.footer: Competitive picture: Zycus is one of two shortlisted vendors (16 April 2026) but the live battle is against do-nothing / deferral: an external spend freeze has paused the project with no restart date.

bullets.text: Non-essential spend frozen due to the conflict/blockade with no restart date; pilot budget unapproved
Close date 31 Aug 2026 not credible: remaining budget-unfreeze, approval, contracting and deployment path cannot complete in 59 days
Economic buyer Bilal Hamadeh has not joined a working session since Oct 2025; deal single-threaded to a champion without budget authority
Scope shrank to a single-BU pilot near Zycus's minimum deal size ('too small for us to be interested')

items: Send Abraham a one-page margin-protection ROI model to justify unfreezing the pilot budget. (by 10 Jul)
Ask Abraham to broker a short call with Bilal to agree the project restart trigger and timing. (by 17 Jul)
Run a value review with Crowe's Ravi to confirm the restart criteria and surface the second vendor. (by 31 Jul)
blocks.title: What moves it forward

contributions.evidence: Stage is ahead of the engagement/evidence the sweep found.

commentary.deal_risk: Risk 32 is driven by Stage is ahead of the engagement/evidence the sweep found; Verdict 'Slowing' — momentum stalling. Only observed negatives count, so these are real warning signs, not gaps.
commentary.win_position: Win 52 is carried by preference strength +0.40 (weight 20); competitive strength +0.50 (weight 15). Offsetting it: momentum 47 vs stage-expected 56.
commentary.deal_momentum: Momentum 47 (roughly flat): Verdict 'Slowing' — momentum stalling. Silence alone was not treated as backward motion.
commentary.evidence_coverage: Early Read: 3 of 7 evidence dimensions seen (engagement, stage integrity, exec and champion). It labels how much of the picture we have, never the primary scores.
commentary.customer_commitment: Commitment sits near the earned-from-zero floor: little observable customer investment surfaced. Commitment is earned, so a low value is honest, not a penalty.
commentary.forecast_confidence: Forecast confidence 28 is anchored on the stage win (52), nudged by commitment 8 and momentum 47, and dragged by risk 32. Read is Early Read — a confidence flag (we know less), not a haircut on the score.

ai_fit_signal.summary: AIS_Score 2.0 / AIS_Status Negative. Product scope has no AI component and the buyer explicitly parked Merlin intake ('I don't see if we would go forward on this, at the moment just pause it') on 30 Oct 2025; the calls confirm the field read rather than contradicting it.

business_case.evidence: Qualitative pain and a margin-protection angle are articulated (materials/fuel-price squeeze, manual onboarding and invoice processing), but no ROI, savings, cost-per-invoice or cycle-time figure has been quantified or agreed.

Amount tidied 148,974 to 150,000 by Carl Kimball
Close date pushed from 30 May 2026 to 31 Aug 2026 and stage moved Formal Evaluation to Shortlisted; forecast set to Best Case
Champion Abraham Mathew hosted Dan in person; confirmed Zycus shortlisted against one other vendor, spend paused

deal_movement.summary: Reads as a rep-side holding pattern on a frozen deal. Recent field moves are administrative (ownership reassigned to a new RVP, a minor amount tidy-up), not buyer progress; the last real buyer engagement was the 18-19 May 2026 in-person meeting and 'wait and watch' email, and the last activity (5 Jun 2026) was a Zycus Horizon invite.

reasons.detail: Khansaheb ($150k, Best Case, Shortlisted, close 31 Aug) has had zero prospect activity for 28 days; the only touches in the window are CRM housekeeping (owner reassignment x2, amount cleanup 148974->150000). The buyer paused all non-essential spend over the conflict/blockade with no restart trigger set, and the deal is single-threaded to champion Abraham Mathew who lacks budget authority while economic buyer Bilal Hamadeh has not joined a session since Oct 2025. If ignored, a forecasted deal quietly slips out of the quarter with no path to reopen the frozen pilot budget.
reasons.metric: 28 days no buyer activity; last real engagement 18 May 2026
reasons.ceo_ask: Ask Mohamad Alhakim why a Best Case $150k deal has been silent 28 days, whether Abraham has any restart-timing commitment from Bilal, and if it should still be forecasted this quarter.
reasons.summary: Forecasted $150k Khansaheb deal frozen and quiet 28 days, no restart date
reasons.evidence: days_since_last_activity=28 as of 2026-07-03; recent_movements_14d are all CRM housekeeping (Owner x2, Amount 148974->150000, Next_Step null->null); pilot budget frozen - 'all non-essential spend is paused until later in the year whilst the conflict/blockade continues'.

items: Buyer 19 May 2026: 'we are on a wait and watch mode. Give it some time, I will get back to you on the way forward.'
No buyer working session since the Oct 2025 POC; Bilal unavailable for both May 2026 meeting attempts.
Last activity 5 Jun 2026 was a rep-side Horizon conference invite, not a buyer response.
confidence_signals.summary: Engagement has thinned to a rep-side holding pattern: buyer in 'wait and watch', no working session since Oct 2025, and the last activity was a Zycus event invite. The relationship is warm but the deal is stalled on an external freeze.

north_star_verdict.math: Close date 2026-08-31 is 59 days out (today 2026-07-03). Shortlisted since 17 Apr 2026; first reached Formal Evaluation 29 Jul 2025, so roughly 11 months in cycle. Last two-way buyer touch was the 18 May in-person meeting plus the 19 May 2026 inbound 'wait and watch' email, about 6 weeks ago; last activity 5 Jun 2026 was a rep-side Horizon invite. With budget frozen and no restart date, the remaining path (budget unfreeze, management approval, contracting, deployment) cannot complete by 31 Aug 2026, so the date is not credible.
evidence: Next Step 18 May 2026: 'all non-essential spend is paused until later in the year whilst the conflict/blockade continues, new construction projects are paused'.
Buyer email 19 May 2026 (Abraham Mathew): 'we are on a wait and watch mode. Give it some time, I will get back to you on the way forward.'
Next Step 16 April 2026: 'confirmed they had shortlisted two vendors and Zycus is one (wouldn't say who else)'.
Forecast is Best Case but the budget is unapproved and frozen and the EB has not joined a working session since Oct 2025, so Best Case is not defensible; honest category is Pipeline.
north_star_verdict.headline: POC-validated deal frozen roughly 8 months by an external non-essential-spend pause; champion Abraham Mathew re-engaged in person on 18 May 2026 but is in 'wait and watch' mode, economic buyer Bilal Hamadeh has not joined a working session since the Oct 2025 POC, and the account was just reassigned to a new RVP owner on 29 Jun 2026.

flags: Deal is single-threaded to Abraham Mathew, who cannot release frozen budget: broker a session with Bilal Hamadeh and finance to reach the budget owner.
No quantified ROI / margin-protection case exists: build a one-page cost-and-savings model Abraham can carry to management to justify unfreezing the pilot.
Evaluation is partner-gated through Crowe (Ravi Shankar) and the second shortlisted vendor is unknown: use the Crowe relationship to surface the rival and confirm the restart trigger.
best_practice_check.summary: This is a POC-validated, single-threaded deal frozen by an external spend pause; winning depends on multi-threading to the budget owner and giving the champion a quantified reason to unfreeze.

customer_preference.evidence: Zycus is one of two shortlisted vendors after a successful onsite POC, but the buyer is currently in 'wait and watch' mode with no active preference expressed (Next Step 16 Apr / 18 May 2026).

competitive_position.summary: Zycus is one of two shortlisted vendors (16 April 2026) but the live battle is against do-nothing / deferral: an external spend freeze has paused the project with no restart date. The single strongest current threat is the spend pause; the undisclosed peer vendor is a secondary, dormant threat because no active bake-off runs while spend is frozen. Win path: reframe procuretech as downturn margin protection, arm the champion with a quantified case, and convert the validated POC into management approval.

competitors.name: Do-nothing / status quo (spend pause due to conflict/blockade)
competitors.quote: all non-essential spend is paused until later in the year whilst the conflict/blockade continues
competitors.how_we_win: Position procuretech as a controllable margin-protection lever in a downturn (Dan's blog plus Agentic AI summit); arm Abraham with a quantified ROI case to justify unfreezing the pilot budget.

ai_positioning_strength.summary: AIS is 2.0 / Negative. The confirmed Phase 1 scope (iSupplier, eInvoicing) is foundational with no Merlin/Agentic component, and no named Khansaheb stakeholder has engaged on any AI conversation across Nov 2025 to Apr 2026. Merlin intake was shown as a value-add on 14 Oct 2025 but the buyer paused it. AI is not the wedge on this deal today.

items.quote: if it is below a certain scale of project, the business will politely walk away
items.criterion: Deal size / commercial threshold (phased single-BU pilot)

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Strong solution fit on the confirmed Phase 1 scope (supplier onboarding, compliance validation, eInvoicing), but expectation friction on deal size and the FTA-portal integration dependency.
